title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
summary: This paper proposes chain-of-thought prompting, which improves complex reasoning in large language models by incorporating intermediate reasoning steps into few-shot examples.

strengths: The method is elegant and simple, improving reasoning without modifying the model
Significant improvements on mathematical and commonsense reasoning tasks
weaknesses: Limited effectiveness on small models, with a clear scale threshold
Chain-of-thought quality is highly dependent on the design of human-crafted examples
Lacks systematic analysis of chain-of-thought correctness
review.final_comment: Chain-of-thought prompting is a practical and influential technique, but its effectiveness is strongly correlated with model scale. This limitation should be noted when applying it.
